what_it_does: Implements the pure write-side authoring backend: it renders JSON specs into Python module source strings for roles, skills, contexts, guides, and flows, validates each generated module by importing it through the real registry in a temporary directory, and loads draft roles from source for dry-run execution.
observations: Maintains a closed field-type registry FIELD_TYPES with scalar, enum, object, and list[object] kinds, plus operator-friendly aliases dict/list[dict].
Recursively renders Pydantic BaseModel output schemas with define-before-use sub-models, conditional Literal imports, and optional/default field modifiers.
output_fields_rows projects a live output_schema class back into authorable rows to protect round-trip edits from silently dropping fields.
render_role_source emits the full ROLE dict using pprint for Python literals and splices output_schema as a bare class reference.
gate_role_source writes the generated module to a temp dir and runs RoleRegistry().discover before any live write.
load_role_from_source imports a draft role module from a temp dir and returns the discovered Role object.
render_entry_source and gate_entry_source handle skills and contexts; _render_guide_source adds a mandatory non-empty grounded_from gate for guides.
render_flow_source and gate_flow_source produce proposed flows with proposes_only=True and a forbidden-pattern floor scan.
Every malformed input raises AuthoringError; there are no silent fallbacks.
standouts: The correctness-gate pattern: every generated artifact is discovered by the real registry in a temp dir before it can reach the live tree.
The recursive schema renderer that emits sub-models in depth-first order and only imports Literal when enums are present.
The mandatory grounding gate for guides that refuses an empty or missing grounded_from list.
The forbidden-substring floor guard for proposed flows.
conventions: Heavy module-level and inline docstrings that state design laws and rationale.
Closed registry with fail-loud rejection of unknown field types and unknown role-schema keys.
Reuse-don't-parallel: gates call the actual registry discover methods rather than duplicating validation logic.
pprint.pformat for Python-literal ROLE dicts; json.dumps only for simple skill/context/guide dicts.
Private helpers prefixed with underscore; public render/gate/load functions.
Type hints and from __future__ import annotations.
concerns: gate_flow_source mutates the process working directory with os.chdir and restores it in finally, which is fragile if another thread or signal interferes.
FORBIDDEN_FLOW_PATTERNS is a naive substring scan that can false-positive on comments, strings, or legitimate identifiers containing forbidden words.
_render_default only checks the top-level type of a default value, so a nested list/dict could still contain non-literal objects that repr would emit.
output_fields_rows maps a plain dict annotation to the string 'dict', which only resolves through the alias to object at render time.
notes: The gated source factory for live cognition artifacts: it turns JSON specs into import-validated Python modules for roles, skills, contexts, guides, and flows.
questions: What are the exact allowed keys in ROLE_FIELDS, ENTRY_FIELDS, and GUIDE_FIELDS?
Where are the live roles/, skills/, contexts/, guides/, and flows/ directories configured and how does Suite call these render/gate functions?
What additional validations do runtime.roles._build_role and runtime.skills._build_entry perform beyond id matching?
Is there any apply/commit helper in this file, or does persistence live entirely in runtime/suite.py?
How are the propose-time model and brief parameters consumed after being stripped here?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file presents itself as the pure write-side renderer and gate, with governance/approval/dispatch intentionally left to Suite, and that separation is reflected in the code.
apparent_themes: Correctness gate outside the live tree
Separation of pure rendering from governed approval
intention_signals: It aims to make agent or operator-authored cognition artifacts safe to land by ensuring every generated module can be discovered by the same registry that will load it later, so one malformed file cannot break the whole cognition layer.
novelty: The combination of a recursive field-to-Pydantic-source renderer with an import-in-tempdir gate for roles, extended uniformly to skills, contexts, guides, and flows in one module.
contribution: A centralized, reusable authoring renderer and correctness gate that a fused system can keep to safely generate and validate roles, skills, contexts, guides, and flows before they touch live registry directories.
summary_for_embedding: A Python module that renders JSON specs into source code for cognition roles, skills, contexts, guides, and flows, then gates each generated module by importing it through the real registry in a temporary directory.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a fail-loud, pure write-side authoring backend that validates generated artifact modules outside the live tree before they are committed.

symbols.does: Exception class for malformed authoring requests.
symbols.description: Raised whenever an authoring input or generated module fails validation; implements the file's fail-loud policy so broken specs never reach the live tree.

symbols.does: Returns the closed field-type registry as operator-facing rows.
symbols.description: Exposes FIELD_TYPES plus aliases with kind, gloss, annotation, and required params; serves as the source of truth for the schema editor dropdown.

symbols.does: Projects a live Pydantic output_schema class back into authorable field rows.
symbols.description: Converts Literal to enum, nested BaseModel to object or list[object], and builtins to registry names; raises AuthoringError for unrepresentable annotations to prevent silent field loss during round-trip edits.

symbols.does: Validates and returns a safe role identifier.
symbols.description: Rejects non-strings, path-like characters, non-identifiers, uppercase, and underscore-prefixed names because the id becomes both file name and ROLE['id'].

symbols.does: Derives the generated output schema class name from a role id.
symbols.description: Splits the id on non-alphanumeric characters, capitalizes each segment, and appends 'Out', for example turning 'my_role' into 'MyRoleOut'.

symbols.does: Converts a field or segment name to PascalCase.
symbols.description: Used to build unique sub-model class names during recursive object and list[object] rendering.

symbols.does: Mutable accumulator for recursive schema rendering.
symbols.description: Tracks generated sub-models in define-before-use order, deduplicates them by class name, and records whether a Literal import is needed.

symbols.does: Initializes the render context.
symbols.description: Sets up an empty sub-model list, an empty seen-name set, and a false literal-import flag.

symbols.does: Adds a generated sub-model if not already emitted.
symbols.description: Ensures each nested object sub-model appears once and in depth-first order so forward references are not needed.

symbols.does: Resolves a declared field type through aliases to its registry row.
symbols.description: Raises AuthoringError for non-string types or unknown types, enforcing the closed field-type registry.

symbols.does: Renders a per-field default to a Python literal via repr.
symbols.description: Accepts only JSON-shaped values such as strings, ints, floats, bools, lists, dicts, and None, and rejects classes, functions, or other non-literals.

symbols.does: Renders one output field into a Pydantic field line.
symbols.description: Handles scalar, enum, object, and list[object] kinds; applies optional and default modifiers; recursively generates sub-models; and raises on duplicate or malformed fields.

symbols.does: Renders the full output field set for the main schema class.
symbols.description: Returns main-class field lines, ordered sub-models, and a Literal-import flag; emits a minimal ok: bool schema when no fields are declared.

symbols.does: Renders a roles/<id>.py module source string from a spec.
symbols.description: Builds the output_schema BaseModel and any sub-models, validates id and fields, maps requires to model_binding, and emits the full ROLE dict with output_schema spliced as a class reference.

symbols.does: Validates a generated role module by discovering it in a temp dir.
symbols.description: Writes the source to a temporary file and runs RoleRegistry().discover; returns an error string on failure or None if the role registers cleanly.

symbols.does: Imports a generated role module from a temp dir and returns its Role.
symbols.description: Used for dry-run execution of a never-written draft; reuses real RoleRegistry discovery and raises AuthoringError on failure.

symbols.does: Validates a skill, context, or guide identifier.
symbols.description: Mirrors _safe_role_id for entries, ensuring the id is a plain lower-case identifier safe to use as a file name.

symbols.does: Renders a skills/<id>.py or contexts/<id>.py module source.
symbols.description: Validates id, content, and known fields; emits a module-level SKILL or CONTEXT dict as a JSON-shaped Python literal.

symbols.does: Renders a guides/<id>.py module source.
symbols.description: Requires non-empty content, target, and grounded_from list; emits a module-level GUIDE dict and enforces mandatory grounding.

symbols.does: Validates a generated skill, context, or guide module by discovering it in a temp dir.
symbols.description: Selects the appropriate registry and runs discover; returns an error string if the entry does not register cleanly.

symbols.does: Renders a proposed flows/<id>.py module source.
symbols.description: Validates id and run() body, builds a params signature, and emits a FLOW dict with proposes_only=True unconditionally.

symbols.does: Validates a proposed flow module for forbidden patterns and loader discovery.
symbols.description: Scans the source for forbidden operator or process-launch substrings, then discovers it with FlowRegistry in a temp directory; returns an error string on any refusal.
